mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation support and risk appetite, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is falling for two consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration forces higher discount-rate expectations and margin pressure, so inflation surprises above recent trend predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month-over-month or the 3-month annualized inflation rate crosses above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPI inflation surprises are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 5-day return after triggers is not significantly below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment signals weakening earnings and demand, so an upward move in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE increases, or if the signal loses directional accuracy in a late-cycle sample.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales RSAFS and payrolls PAYEMS both strengthen, the market reads it as durable domestic demand, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal has no incremental predictive power over either series alone, or if 20-day returns are not positive in the top-quartile joint-demand regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial activity and stronger cyclical earnings, so higher industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO uptrends are followed by non-positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the directional hit rate falls below 50% over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST signals future construction, labor demand, and spending spillovers, supporting equity returns with a lag over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises at least 5% above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts are not followed by higher 60-day SPX returns than baseline, or if the signal reverses sign in more than one-third of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market softening captured by falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE depresses earnings expectations and risk sentiment, so the combined labor deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 months while UNRATE rises in the same period
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, the combination of FEDFUNDS above neutral and CPIAUCSL still elevated signals persistent real-rate pressure and valuation compression, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation state does not produce lower 60-day SPX returns than the non-trigger state, or if the signal loses significance after accounting for either proxy separately.
